english: 1. instrum. of ču (chu); Sch. also čús-ɣtoṅ-ba (chus gtong ba) 'to melt'; more correctly: to gild, to plate (in the warm or in the cold way), to overspread with a gold or silver liquid. — after five days C., W., or, the present day included, on the sixth day, cog. to bču (bcu).
such a one, lo čé-ge-mo żig-la (lo che ge mo zhig la) in such and such a year Dzl.; čé-ge-mo kyoḍ (che ge mo khyod) you so and so Thgr.
W. being jealous of one's own honour, ni f.

arrogance, haughtiness, sde-pai če-tábs (sde pa'i che thabs) the arrogance of the great Ma.; *če-tábs-med-kan* (*che thabs med mkhan*) W. affable, condescending, kind; če-tábs-čan (che thabs can) proud, arrogant, haughty; če-tábs byéḍ-pa (che thabs byed pa) B., *čo-če* (*co che*) W., *zuṅ-wa* (*zung ba*) C., to be arrogant, haughty.
a missive to an inferior, an edict.

1. adj. great, (for čén-po (chen po)); bu čé-ba (bu che ba) the eldest son, the elder; čé-bar gyúr-ba (che bar gyur ba) to become great or greater, to grow, increase e.g. of passions; čé-bar gró-ba (che bar gro ba), čer gró-ba (cher gro ba) id., cf. čer (cher), čé-bar byéḍ-pa (che bar byed pa) to make great; frq. in conjunction with nouns: że-sdáṅ čé-ba (zhe sdang che ba) great with respect to anger, i.e. very prone to anger; rigs čé-żiṅ (rigs che zhing) being of high extraction; also in compounds, v. below. — 2. sbst. greatness, high degree Mil.; superiority, excellence, ... kyi čé-ba stón-pa (... kyi che ba ston pa) to show the superiority of a thing Mil.; *čé-wa sruṅ-wa* (*che ba srung ba*) Ld. to behave decently, respectably. — 3. vb., pf. čes (ches) to be great, not only in čé-żiṅ (che zhing), čé-o (che'o), but also in: ṅa-rgyál ma čé-żig (nga rgyal ma che zhig) do not be great in pride, i.e. do not allow pride to become great Mil., and so in similar cases; cf. čes (ches). Comp. čé-ka (che ka) Sch.: 'chiefly; the plurality. — če-kyáḍ (che khyad) greatness, size Dzl. — *če-ḍál* (*che dal*) W., *če-dál-la čud soṅ* (*che dal la chud song*) he has entered the class of adults, he has come to full age. — čé-rgyu (che rgyu) = čé-ba (che ba), cf. rgyu (rgyu) 3. — če-brgyúḍ (che brgyud) perh. lineage by the eldest sons Glr. — če-čúṅ (che chung) great and small; size; če-čúṅ ni (che chung ni) in size — če-dón (che don) the coming to full age Mil. nt. — *če-mí* (*che mi*) W. an adult. — *če-lóṅs* (*che longs*) C. grown up, adult (Sch. 'chiefly'?)
one's elder sister Cs., the elder wife Sch.
Lìs. = bden-dpáṅ (bden dpang), witness, eye-witness; witness, testimony; če-bżir drís-te (che bzhir dris te) being questioned as a witness, or asked for a testimony Stg.

as sbst. of rare occurrence, Schr.: reason, signification = don (don); Sch.: čed čén-po (ched chen po) a great thing, an important business or affair. Mostly čéd-du (ched du) postp. c. genit. on account of, because of, for; ltá-bai čéd-du yin (lta ba'i ched du yin) it is in order to see Pth.; rín-gyi čéd-du (rin gyi ched du) as an equivalent Pth.; lhai čéd-du ₒdzin-pa (lha'i ched du 'dzin pa) or ynyér-ba (gnyer ba), also rjés-su ₒdzin-pa (rjes su 'dzin pa) or ynyér-ba (gnyer ba) to admit to the discipleship or communion of a god Mil. — As an adv. čed-du (ched du) seems to signify 1. for a certain purpose, designedly, purposely, expressly, e.g. with byéḍ-pa (byed pa) to do, to make a thing; mṅágs-pa (mngags pa) to send off, dispatch. — 2. again, once more, once again, = pyir (phyir) Mil. — dgos-čéd (dgos ched) the construction of any noun with pyir-du (phyir du), čéd-du (ched du) etc., regarded by Tib. grammarians as a case of declension.

B. and C., *čén-mo* (*chen mo*) W., great (čén-mo (chen mo) in B. only as fem. Dzl. Nro, b), čén-por gyúr-ba (chen por gyur ba), W. *čen čá-če* (*chen cha che*), to become great, to increase, col. also for to grow up; skyes-bu čén-po (skyes bu chen po) a great man, a man of great worth (by his talents and actions), a saint; *mi čén-mo* (*mi chen mo*) W. a man of quality, of rank, a nobleman, a rich man; čén-ma (chen ma) the first wife in rank; čen čuṅ ynyis (chen chung gnyis) the first and second wife Glr.; *čén-mo* (*chen mo*) W. also: old, *ṭ'ú-gu lo ču čen-mo* (*phru gu lo bcu chen mo*) a child ten years old.
Lex., Cs. stillness, silence, čém-mer ₒdúg-pa (chem mer 'dug pa) Schr., čém-mer ₒkód-pa (chem mer 'kod pa) C., to sit still without speaking.

in compounds: 1. čaṅ-čém(s) (chang chem(s)) v. čaṅ (chang). — 2. k'a-čém(s) (kha chem(s)), resp. żal-čéms(s) (zhal chems(s)), bka-čém(s) (bka' chem(s)) farewell exhortation; last will, testament, srás-la k'a- (sras la kha-)
